remember: Students will be able to define recursion and distinguish it from iteration.
Students will be able to explain the concept of recursive function calls and backtracking.
Students will be able to identify and write recursive algorithms for simple problems.
understand: Students will be able to understand the conditions for a recursive function to terminate.
Students will be able to explain the concept of base case and recursive case in a recursive function.
Students will be able to identify and explain the time and space complexity of recursive algorithms.
apply: Students will be able to write recursive functions in programming languages like Java, C++, and Python.
Students will be able to use recursion to solve problems like factorial, Fibonacci series, and binary search.
Students will be able to optimize recursive algorithms for better performance.
analyze: Students will be able to compare the efficiency of recursive and iterative solutions for a given problem.
Students will be able to identify and fix stack overflow errors in recursive algorithms.
Students will be able to optimize recursive algorithms using techniques like memoization and dynamic programming.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of recursion and its applications.
To learn how to write recursive functions in programming languages.
To analyze and optimize recursive algorithms for better performance.
board_exam_objectives: To recall the definition and types of recursion.
To identify and write recursive algorithms for simple problems.
To analyze and optimize recursive algorithms for better performance.
